name: python3-rsa
title: python3-rsa
description:
Pure-Python RSA implementation (Python 3)
Python-RSA is a pure-Python RSA implementation. It supports encryption
and decryption, signing and verifying signatures, and key generation
according to PKCS#1 version 1.5. It can be used as a Python library as
well as on the commandline. The code was mostly written by Sybren A. Stüvel.
.
This package contains the module for Python 3.

commands.name: py3rsa-priv2pub
commands.description: read a private key and output the corresponding public key

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use py3rsa-decrypt because Pure-Python RSA implementation (Python 3).
When the engagement is in the install phase, use py3rsa-decrypt as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `py3rsa-decrypt [OPTION]... PRIVATE_KEY` as documented upstream for py3rsa-decrypt.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair py3rsa-decrypt with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `py3rsa-decrypt --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.

documentation.how_to_use: Install/setup: sudo apt install python-rsa. Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `py3rsa-decrypt [OPTION]... PRIVATE_KEY`. Pure-Python RSA implementation (Python 3).
documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'install' phase against surfaces [local] in the Kali Linux category. Fits when the engagement needs: Pure-Python RSA implementation (Python 3).
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool because: Pure-Python RSA implementation (Python 3).
usage_sections: Install with: sudo apt install python-rsa
Install with: sudo apt install python-rsa
